title: Fix incorrect multipler for limit field
body:
I want to merge this change because we recently introduced the `values` field with input limit. By accident the query cost was added to incorrect place. This PR fixes this. 

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> The changes on `SelectedAttribute` is expected. We don't have `limit` there, and it is not paginated.  (The query cost for this field will be handled separatly)

Impact checklist: no migrations/api changes/etc marked.

functions_modified.full_name: test(s) to add for Attribute.values cost multiplier

step_plan.summary: Fix GraphQL query complexity calculation by applying the `limit` multiplier to `Attribute.values(limit: Int)` and removing the incorrect multiplier from `SelectedAttribute.values`. Add regression tests to ensure query cost scales with `limit` only where the argument exists.

steps.operation: Move the query-cost multiplier to the correct GraphQL field by adding `multipliers: ["limit"]` to the `Attribute.values` COST_MAP entry (and ensuring its base cost remains unchanged).
steps.reason: `Attribute.values(limit: Int)` is the field that actually accepts the `limit` argument, so query complexity must scale with `limit` there; otherwise expensive queries can slip past the max complexity validation.
steps.side_effects: Queries using `Attribute.values(limit: N)` may be rejected more often under `GRAPHQL_QUERY_MAX_COMPLEXITY` because their computed cost will now correctly increase with `N`; no schema/resolver changes.

steps.operation: Remove the incorrect `multipliers: ["limit"]` configuration from the `SelectedAttribute.values` COST_MAP entry so its cost is constant for now.
steps.reason: `SelectedAttribute.values` does not define a `limit` argument and is not paginated, so applying a `limit` multiplier is both incorrect and potentially breaks cost calculation/validation assumptions for that field (and can also mislead future maintenance).
steps.side_effects: Queries using `SelectedAttribute.values` will no longer have any cost scaling based on a non-existent argument; overall complexity may decrease for those queries; future work may add a different cost model for this field as noted in the PR body.

steps.operation: Add/adjust query-cost validation tests to assert that `Attribute.values(limit: N)` increases computed query cost with `N`, and that `SelectedAttribute.values` does not apply a `limit` multiplier.
steps.function_to_modify: test(s) to add for Attribute.values cost multiplier
steps.reason: Provides regression coverage for the bug (multiplier placed on the wrong type) and locks in the intended behavior: only the field that accepts `limit` should be multiplied by it.
steps.side_effects: May require updating expected complexity numbers/snapshots if existing tests implicitly relied on the previous (incorrect) cost map; tests should be written to compare relative costs (e.g., limit=1 vs limit=10) to reduce brittleness.
